exact.description: Only the live milestone should be exact.

structured.description: The next 2-3 milestones or versions should be specifically structured but still revisable.

horizon.description: Farther milestones keep intent exact while phase detail stays light.

current_frontier.next_action: Assay/specimen quality failure-mode checklist is complete and wired into measurement, glossary, residual-disease modality extraction, model-output wrapper, validator, machine-representation source extraction, navigation, and ORP boundaries. The next no-outreach public-source successor, if selected, is measurement-state-refusal-fixture-extension-v0. Active state remains machine-representation-expert-validation-human-authorization-blocker-v0; actual expert-validation execution, issue operations, outreach, response intake, claim upgrade, clinical interpretation, model-governance clearance, publication, real-report quality review, and private-lab work remain blocked unless a human explicitly authorizes scope or selects another named no-outreach public-source phase.

versions.label: Case Intake Frontier
versions.intent: Create the public-safe caregiver case intake foundation for future private multiple myeloma case work.

milestones.label: Caregiver Case Intake Frontier v0
success_criteria: Caregiver intake phase is canonical, linked, cataloged, and public-safe.
No real case data, identifiers, records, uploads, or patient-specific advice appear in public artifacts.
Clawdad has a concrete next build target after the product spec and synthetic fixture.

phases.label: Caregiver Intake Product Spec v0
phases.goal: Make caregiver-friendly multiple myeloma case intake a concrete public-safe phase with a product spec, synthetic fixture, and private handoff boundary.
requirements: Public artifacts only: no real case data, PHI, uploaded records, private notes, identifiers, or dates tied to a person.
Keep the interface caregiver-friendly: plain language, unknown/not sure states, unit/date helpers, save/resume concept, and final review before private handoff.
Block diagnosis, prognosis, treatment recommendations, trial recommendations, eligibility guidance, monitoring advice, urgency guidance, and expanded-access guidance.
success_criteria: Caregiver Case Intake Product Spec v0 exists and is linked from roadmap, README, case-to-cure blueprint, inventory, and catalog.
Synthetic caregiver intake fixture exists and is visibly artificial.
Next autonomous build target is explicit and public-safe.
plans: Create the no-PHI public product/spec artifact and synthetic fixture.
Then delegate a private-intake schema/projection/checklist or synthetic prototype slice.

versions.label: Case-To-Cure Adaptive Master Plan
versions.intent: Run the multiple myeloma case-to-cure initiative as a durable adaptive plan: complete one public-safe step, synthesize the next step from the result, queue it, and keep moving until the master completion audit is complete or externally blocked.

milestones.label: Case-To-Cure Adaptive Master Plan v0
success_criteria: The adaptive master plan is canonical, linked, cataloged, and public-safe.
Every completed step produces a completion handoff and a next-step synthesis before the loop stops.
The active ORP additional queue has pending public-safe work until the master completion audit is reached or blocked by an explicit human gate.

phases.label: Case-To-Cure Loop Governor v0
phases.goal: Make the public multiple myeloma case-to-cure plan durable and adaptive: each completed step must inspect its outcome, synthesize the next safest public step, and queue or block that step explicitly.
requirements: Use disease-programs/multiple-myeloma/case-to-cure-adaptive-master-plan-v0.md as the canonical plan.
Do not mark the initiative complete when a narrow phase closes; read the handoff and choose the next public-safe step.
No real case data, PHI, clinical advice, treatment ranking, trial advice, or cure claim may enter public outputs.
success_criteria: ORP live frontier points at the adaptive master plan and a durable additional queue contains the next public-safe work.
The delegation packet requires next-step synthesis after each completed item.
Validation passes after navigation and metadata updates.
plans: Create the master plan artifact, wire ORP, update Clawdad brief, validate, then start the next delegation run.

phases.label: Private Intake Schema Contract v0
phases.goal: Define the private-only intake record shape and public-safe projection boundary for future consented multiple myeloma case work.
requirements: Document field groups, unknown states, provenance, private-only identifiers, and public projection rules without collecting real data.
Preserve caregiver-friendly unknown/not-sure states, final review, consent/privacy boundaries, clinician-review boundaries, and no-advice boundaries.

Do not define a live backend, collect real case data, publish free-text case details, or create diagnosis, prognosis, treatment, trial, monitoring, urgency, expanded-access, ranking, or cure-claim outputs.
success_criteria: A public-safe contract or blocker note exists and links to the caregiver intake spec, projection checklist, synthetic fixture, case-to-cure blueprint, and private-to-public workflow.
The ORP additional queue records what the contract revealed and activates or blocks the next public-safe item.
Validation passes after metadata, catalog, and navigation updates.
plans: Prefer a markdown contract first; executable schema can follow only after the public boundary is clear.

phases.label: Static Synthetic Caregiver Prototype v0
phases.goal: Design the first synthetic-only public prototype plan for a caregiver-centered intake flow that never accepts real case details.
requirements: Use synthetic fixtures only and keep emergency, clinician-review, consent, privacy, and no-advice copy visible.
Reference the private intake schema contract for field groups, unknown states, provenance prompts, and public projection outcomes.

Do not create a live backend, collect real case data, request uploads, publish free-text case details, or generate diagnosis, prognosis, treatment, trial, monitoring, urgency, expanded-access, ranking, or cure-claim outputs.
success_criteria: A static synthetic prototype plan or fixture extension exists with public navigation and validation coverage.
The prototype plan uses visibly artificial data only and blocks public submission.
The ORP additional queue records what the prototype revealed and activates or blocks the next public-safe item.
plans: Keep it static and synthetic before any backend or data capture.

phases.label: Public Projection Validator v0
phases.goal: Create a fail-closed public projection validator or validator spec for no-PHI, no-advice, synthetic-only public exports.
requirements: Validator must reject identifiers, real case free text, advice language, rankings, urgency guidance, and publication without gate approval.
Use the private intake schema contract, projection checklist, and static synthetic prototype plan as the public rule sources.
Start as protocol-level checks if executable validation needs a later schema.
success_criteria: A validator, validator map, or precise blocker note exists and is linked from case-intake and case-to-cure artifacts.
The validator path remains synthetic-only and cannot process real case data or generate patient-specific outputs.
The ORP additional queue records what the validator revealed and activates or blocks the next public-safe item.
plans: Start as protocol-level checks if executable validation needs a later schema.

phases.label: Consent Privacy Security Retention Gate v0
phases.goal: Specify the human and technical gates before any real case intake, storage, retention, emergency handling, or clinician review.
requirements: Public artifact must define gates and blockers only; it must not authorize real intake or collect data.
Use the public projection validator to name what remains blocked before any future implementation.
Do not create a live backend, consent workflow, storage system, emergency handler, or clinical review process.
success_criteria: Gate spec exists or blocker note records the exact private, legal, clinical, and security reviews needed.
The ORP additional queue records what the gate revealed and activates or blocks the next public-safe item.
Validation passes after metadata, catalog, and navigation updates.
plans: Tie to private-to-public workflow, publication gate, and caregiver intake boundary.

phases.label: Case Feature Normalization Contract v0
phases.goal: Define the public-safe shape for normalized disease-state and context features used by the private case-to-cure pipeline.
requirements: Include unknown, not-tested, source-missing, provenance, timepoint, and limitation fields without producing prognosis or option ranking.
Use the consent/privacy/security/retention gate and public projection validator as fail-closed boundaries.

Do not create a fillable public case-feature schema, real case upload, patient-specific interpretation, prognosis, eligibility guidance, treatment guidance, trial guidance, monitoring guidance, or cure-claim output.
success_criteria: A normalization contract, schema plan, or blocker note exists and links to the case-feature public summary, projection validator, consent gate, and case-to-cure blueprint.
The contract is shape-only and cannot process or normalize a real person.
The ORP additional queue records what the contract revealed and activates or blocks the next public-safe item.
plans: Keep this as shape and boundary first; executable schema follows only after field semantics are stable.

phases.label: Measurement Normalization Contract v0
phases.goal: Define public-safe MRD, response, relapse, lab, imaging, method, threshold, sample, and timepoint normalization for private case work.
requirements: Preserve method, threshold, sample, timepoint, durability, endpoint role, source context, and uncertainty.
Use the MRD glossary, endpoint language guardrail addendum, case-feature normalization contract, and projection validator as fail-closed boundaries.
Do not create cure claims, prognosis, treatment guidance, trial guidance, eligibility guidance, monitoring guidance, urgency guidance, or patient-specific interpretation.
success_criteria: Measurement contract or blocker note links to the MRD glossary, endpoint guardrails, case-feature normalization contract, and case-to-cure blueprint.
MRD, response, relapse, lab, and imaging terms preserve method, threshold, sample, timepoint, durability, endpoint role, source context, uncertainty, and limitations.
The ORP additional queue records what the contract revealed and activates or blocks the next public-safe item.
plans: Use current MRD guardrails as the boundary before creating any fixture or schema update.

phases.label: Therapy Exposure Timeline Contract v0
phases.goal: Define the private timeline shape for prior therapies, lines, exposures, responses, toxicities, constraints, and refractory context.
requirements: Preserve therapy class, exposure status, line or timing context, response linkage, toxicity or constraint labels, source status, timepoint buckets, and limitations.
Use the treatment-class taxonomy, immune therapy access boundary, measurement normalization contract, and projection validator as fail-closed boundaries.
Do not infer recommendations, eligibility, sequencing, ranking, trial fit, monitoring guidance, urgency guidance, or patient-specific interpretation.
success_criteria: Therapy exposure contract or blocker note links to treatment taxonomy, immune therapy boundary, measurement normalization contract, and case-to-cure blueprint.
Therapy exposure fields preserve source status, timepoint buckets, response linkage, toxicity or constraint labels, uncertainty, and limitations without exact dates or identifiers.
The ORP additional queue records what the contract revealed and activates or blocks the next public-safe item.
plans: Keep dates and identifiers private; public examples must be synthetic and non-identifying.

phases.label: Molecular Immune Context Contract v0
phases.goal: Define the private/public boundary for cytogenetics, genomics, target assays, pathology, flow, immune context, and source validity fields.
requirements: Preserve cytogenetics, genomics, target assays, pathology, flow, immune context, assay/source validity, timepoint buckets, uncertainty, review status, and limitations.

Use the case-feature normalization contract, therapy exposure timeline contract, mechanism maps, context modifier map, measurement normalization contract, and projection validator as fail-closed boundaries.
Do not infer actionability, test need, target fit, treatment guidance, trial guidance, eligibility guidance, monitoring guidance, urgency guidance, ranking, or patient-specific interpretation.
success_criteria: Molecular immune context contract or blocker note links to mechanism maps, context modifier map, source registry, therapy exposure timeline contract, and case-to-cure blueprint.
Molecular and immune fields preserve source, method, timepoint bucket, uncertainty, review status, and limitations without real values, report text, or identifiers.
The ORP additional queue records what the contract revealed and activates or blocks the next public-safe item.
plans: Capture shape, provenance, and uncertainty before any hypothesis review uses the fields.

phases.label: Evidence Retrieval Packet v0
phases.goal: Define a reproducible public-source evidence packet skeleton for future private case questions without patient matching.
requirements: Preserve source IDs, query records, source freshness checks, access dates, limitations, uncertainty, no-completeness warnings, and no-advice boundaries.
Use the measurement normalization contract, therapy exposure timeline contract, molecular immune context contract, source registry, query protocols, and publication gate as fail-closed boundaries.
Do not match a person to evidence, trials, products, targets, therapies, access routes, eligibility, monitoring, prognosis, ranking, or patient-specific interpretation.
success_criteria: Evidence packet skeleton or blocker note links to source registry, ClinicalTrials.gov query protocol, open research maps, and case-to-cure blueprint.
The skeleton preserves source freshness, query provenance, limitation fields, uncertainty, and no-completeness warnings without matching or advising for a person.
The ORP additional queue records what the packet revealed and activates or blocks the next public-safe item.
plans: Do not match, rank, or advise; make the retrieval method auditable.

phases.label: Trial Therapy Landscape Non-Advice Gate v0
phases.goal: Define how trial, therapy, product, target, and access context can be extracted as landscape information without advice.
requirements: Preserve trial, therapy, product, target, jurisdiction, source freshness, access-date, status, limitation, uncertainty, and review fields as landscape context only.

Use the evidence retrieval packet, source registry, ClinicalTrials.gov query protocol, trial-landscape schema, therapy-record schema, immune therapy boundary, therapy exposure timeline contract, and publication gate as fail-closed boundaries.
Do not infer availability, eligibility, sequencing, treatment guidance, trial guidance, expanded-access guidance, monitoring guidance, urgency guidance, ranking, or patient-specific interpretation.
success_criteria: Trial therapy landscape non-advice gate or blocker note links to the evidence retrieval packet, source registry, query protocol, trial-landscape schema, therapy-record schema, and immune therapy boundary.
Landscape fields preserve source freshness, access-date state, jurisdiction, status, uncertainty, and limitations without patient matching or advice.
The ORP additional queue records what the gate revealed and activates or blocks the next public-safe item.
plans: Date-stamp status and jurisdiction fields; route patient-specific questions to clinical teams.

phases.label: Candidate Hypothesis Review Question Set v0
phases.goal: Represent candidate hypotheses as source-scoped review questions for qualified humans, not recommendations, rankings, or patient-action outputs.
requirements: Preserve evidence packet IDs, landscape gate status, source context, uncertainty, review lens, blocked uses, and explicit no patient-action output.
Use the evidence retrieval packet, trial therapy landscape non-advice gate, candidate-option scoring rubric, review packet templates, source registry, and publication gate as fail-closed boundaries.

Do not infer recommendations, rankings, treatment guidance, trial guidance, expanded-access guidance, monitoring guidance, urgency guidance, availability, eligibility, or patient-specific interpretation.
success_criteria: Question-set artifact or blocker note links to the evidence retrieval packet, trial therapy landscape non-advice gate, candidate-option scoring rubric, and review packet templates.
Candidate hypotheses are framed only as source-scoped questions for qualified human review, never patient options or ranked choices.
The ORP additional queue records what the question set revealed and activates or blocks the next public-safe item.
plans: Completed as Candidate Hypothesis Review Question Set v0; next safest public step is a multidisciplinary review packet builder skeleton that can copy, reference, omit, or refuse question fields without generating claims or patient-specific outputs.

phases.label: Multidisciplinary Review Packet Builder v0
phases.goal: Define the public-safe packet builder skeleton and route rules for multidisciplinary review under private and clinical gates.
requirements: Preserve question IDs, artifact IDs, source IDs, review-lens fields, missing-input blockers, safety boundaries, and no-generated-claims behavior.

Use the candidate hypothesis review question set, review-packet template, review-packet builder manifest spec, route-table schema/tool, evidence retrieval packet, trial/therapy landscape non-advice gate, publication gate, source registry, public projection validator, private intake schema contract, and public safety workflow as boundaries.

Do not assemble real packets, process real case facts, generate biomedical prose, publish patient-specific outputs, recommend, rank, match trials, infer eligibility, infer availability, provide expanded-access or monitoring guidance, authorize publication, or make clinical decisions.
success_criteria: Packet builder skeleton, route rules, or blocker note links to the candidate hypothesis review question set, manifest spec, review-packet template, route-table schema/tool, evidence packet, landscape gate, source registry, and publication gate.
Route behavior is limited to copy, reference, omit, refuse, or mark missing fields; it does not create generated claims, recommendations, rankings, or patient-specific outputs.
The ORP additional queue records what the builder revealed and activates or blocks the next public-safe item.
plans: Completed as Multidisciplinary Review Packet Builder v0; next safest public step is an expert validation loop that treats issues, outreach roles, and response-ledger states as public-safe dispositions only.

phases.label: Expert Validation Loop v0
phases.goal: Make expert validation issue updates and response dispositions part of the recurring public loop without publishing private correspondence or implying expert review is complete.
requirements: Tie public issue statuses, outreach-map roles, response-ledger dispositions, source correction tasks, and review-lens gaps into the recurring adaptive loop.
Represent any expert response content only as safe dispositions unless public permission and public source context exist.
Preserve issue IDs, artifact IDs, source IDs, review-lens fields, allowed dispositions, next public actions, uncertainty, and blocked-use boundaries.

Do not publish private correspondence, unpublished expert comments, reviewer identity disclosures beyond already-public outreach maps, recommendations, rankings, patient-specific outputs, treatment guidance, trial guidance, expanded-access guidance, monitoring guidance, publication authorization, or clinical decisions.
success_criteria: Expert validation loop artifact or blocker note links to issue index, outreach map, response ledger, candidate question set, multidisciplinary builder, source registry, publication gate, and public safety workflow.
All expert validation updates are represented as public-safe statuses, allowed dispositions, source-context-needed notes, or next public tasks without copying private correspondence.
The ORP additional queue records what the expert validation loop revealed and activates or blocks the next public-safe item.
plans: Completed as Expert Validation Loop v0; next safest public step is the case-to-public learning extraction gate.

phases.label: Case-To-Public Learning Extraction Gate v0
phases.goal: Define the sanitation, aggregation, source-scope, uncertainty, privacy, review, and publication gate for any future public learning extracted from a private case.
requirements: Only approved, sanitized, non-identifying, non-advisory, source-scoped learning may become public.

Preserve artifact IDs, source IDs, issue IDs, review-lens fields, disposition states, privacy decision state, allowed output type, uncertainty, limitations, blocked-use boundaries, and publication-gate state.
Use the expert validation loop, publication gate, private-to-public workflow, public safety rules, source registry, stage validator map, and pipeline blueprint as boundaries.

Block patient identifiers, raw records, dates tied to a person, single-case claims, private correspondence, unpublished expert comments, patient-specific recommendations, rankings, treatment guidance, trial guidance, expanded-access guidance, monitoring guidance, publication authorization, and clinical decisions.
success_criteria: Learning extraction gate or blocker note links to the expert validation loop, publication gate, private-to-public workflow, public safety rules, source registry, stage validator map, and pipeline blueprint.
Gate fields preserve allowed output type, privacy decision state, public source context, uncertainty, limitations, review status, blocked uses, and publication-gate state.
The ORP additional queue records what the extraction gate revealed and activates or blocks the next public-safe item.
plans: Completed as Case-To-Public Learning Extraction Gate v0; next safest public step is an end-to-end synthetic case dry run that exercises success, omit, refusal, and blocker paths without real case material.

phases.label: End-To-End Synthetic Case Dry Run v0
phases.goal: Dry-run the complete case-to-cure path with synthetic-only artifacts from caregiver intake through publication gate.
requirements: Use visibly artificial data only and exercise success, omit, refusal, and blocker paths across intake, projection, normalization, evidence retrieval, landscape gating, candidate question routing, review-packet route rules, expert validation disposition plumbing, case-to-public learning extraction, and publication-gate refusal.

Link the synthetic caregiver intake fixture, synthetic case-to-cure pipeline, public projection validator, normalization contracts, evidence retrieval packet, trial/therapy landscape non-advice gate, candidate hypothesis review question set, multidisciplinary review packet builder, expert validation loop, case-to-public learning extraction gate, publication gate, stage validator map, pipeline blueprint, source registry, private-to-public workflow, and public safety rules.

Do not use real case data, identifiers, raw records, uploads, person-linked dates, free-text case details, private correspondence, patient-specific outputs, recommendations, rankings, matching, treatment guidance, trial guidance, expanded-access guidance, monitoring guidance, publication authorization, or clinical decisions.
success_criteria: Synthetic dry-run artifact or validation report exists and links all owner artifacts, gates, and blockers.
The dry run demonstrates success, omit, refusal, and blocker paths without processing or resembling a real person.
The ORP additional queue records what the dry run revealed and activates or blocks the master completion audit.
plans: Completed as End-To-End Synthetic Case Dry Run v0; next safest public step is the case-to-cure master completion audit.

phases.label: Case-To-Cure Master Completion Audit v0
phases.goal: Audit whether the adaptive master plan is complete, safely blocked, or ready for a new named phase.
requirements: Inspect the adaptive master backlog, public artifacts, catalogs, navigation, ORP state, validators, blockers, and remaining human gates.

Do not call the initiative complete unless all remaining work is explicitly expert-review-needed, private-lab-needed, clinical-team-needed, legal-needed, regulatory-needed, publication-gate-needed, human-review-needed, or out-of-scope.
Do not use real case data, clinical judgment, outreach, private lab access, publication authorization, or patient-specific guidance.
success_criteria: Completion audit records files changed, validation state, blockers, and the next named phase or human gate.
The audit states whether the public-safe adaptive scope is complete, safely blocked, or ready for a new named phase.
The ORP additional queue records the audit outcome and next state without requiring real case data or human decisions.
plans: Completed as Case-To-Cure Master Completion Audit v0; next state is blocked on named human gates unless a human selects a new named public-safe phase.

phases.label: Case-To-Cure Public-Scope Human Gate Blocker v0
phases.goal: Block further autonomous case-to-cure work until a human selects a new named public-safe phase or clears a named gate.
requirements: Stop autonomous case-to-cure backlog work unless a human selects a new named public-safe phase or clears a named gate.
Keep remaining work labeled as expert-review-needed, private-lab-needed, clinical-team-needed, legal-needed, regulatory-needed, publication-gate-needed, human-review-needed, or out-of-scope.
Do not create another sibling artifact, tool, schema, or extraction phase by momentum.
Do not use real case data, clinical judgment, outreach, private lab access, publication authorization, credentials, paid services, or patient-specific guidance.
success_criteria: The blocker is explicit in ORP state, additional queue, version stack, inventory, and completion audit.
The next action names human gate classes instead of an autonomous artifact.
Validation passes with the completion audit cataloged.
plans: Blocked until human selection of a new named public-safe phase or clearance of expert-review-needed, private-lab-needed, clinical-team-needed, legal-needed, regulatory-needed, publication-gate-needed, or human-review-needed gates.

phases.label: Public Caregiver Intake Frontend v0
phases.goal: Created and smoke-tested the public-facing, static, synthetic-only caregiver intake frontend prototype for multiple myeloma case organization without collecting real patient data or providing medical advice.
requirements: Use generic caregiver language only; do not use private names or private relationship details.
Build only a static/synthetic public prototype in this repo: no real data collection, no uploads, no backend, no auth, no database, no email submission, and no patient-identifying data.

Use the existing caregiver case intake product spec, private intake schema contract, projection checklist, consent/privacy/security gate, synthetic caregiver fixture, public safety governance, and private-to-public workflow as boundaries.

The interface must be plain-language, caregiver-friendly, progressive, layout-stable, and explicit about unknown/not sure answers, consent/privacy boundaries, emergency boundaries, clinician review, and no medical advice.
success_criteria: A static frontend artifact exists and can be opened or served locally without sending data anywhere.
The frontend uses synthetic fixture data only and visibly warns against entering real patient information.
The public repo navigation points to the prototype and validation passes.
A smoke-test report records no-submit, no-storage, no-upload, no external image dependency, responsive layout, and no-advice checks before any future frontend iteration.
plans: Completed as Static Synthetic Caregiver Intake Frontend v0 and Static Synthetic Caregiver Intake Frontend Smoke Test v0.
Next active public-safe phase is myeloma-machine-representation-implementation-v0.

phases.label: Myeloma Machine Representation Implementation v0
phases.goal: Turn the myeloma machine representation architecture into synthetic-only, public-safe schema and fixture artifacts without model weights, real case data, patient-specific predictions, treatment advice, trial advice, or option ranking.
requirements: Use the machine representation stack, case-feature normalization, measurement normalization, therapy exposure timeline, molecular/immune context, evidence retrieval, and public safety governance as boundaries.
Define source context, tumor genomic events, transcriptome state, marrow ecosystem state, clinical context, treatment timeline, measurement timeline, missingness, uncertainty, and review status.
Use synthetic examples only; do not use real case data, identifiers, raw records, uploads, exact person-linked dates, private notes, or free-text case details.
Do not produce model weights, predictions, prognosis, treatment advice, trial advice, monitoring guidance, urgency guidance, matching, ranking, or patient-specific outputs.
success_criteria: A public myeloma-state-object-schema-v0 artifact exists with metadata and navigation links.
A synthetic-myeloma-state-fixture-v0 artifact exercises complete multimodal, missing RNA, missing single-cell, and private-source-blocked scenarios.
A model-output-boundary-wrapper-v0 artifact defines refusal and uncertainty rules for progression, response, MRD, and resistance head placeholders.
A myeloma-state-validator-rule-map-v0 artifact maps schema, fixture, and wrapper rules to fail-closed public checks.
A machine-representation-source-extraction-v0 artifact maps architecture claims to source IDs, source-context gaps, uncertainty, and expert-review needs.
A machine-representation-source-gap-task-queue-v0 artifact turns source-context gaps into contribution-ready public tasks.
A machine-representation-source-gap-issue-draft-packet-v0 artifact prepares human-reviewable issue draft text without autonomous issue creation or outreach.
No real case data, identifiers, raw records, exact dates, report text, uploads, model weights, patient-specific predictions, recommendations, matching, ranking, or clinical decisions are introduced.
Validation passes after catalog and ORP updates.
plans: Completed myeloma-state-object-schema-v0 as schemas/myeloma-state-object-schema-v0.md.
Completed synthetic-myeloma-state-fixture-v0 as examples/synthetic-myeloma-state-fixture-v0.json.
Completed model-output-boundary-wrapper-v0 as disease-programs/multiple-myeloma/model-output-boundary-wrapper-v0.md.
Completed myeloma-state-validator-rule-map-v0 as disease-programs/multiple-myeloma/myeloma-state-validator-rule-map-v0.md.
Completed machine-representation-source-extraction-v0 as disease-programs/multiple-myeloma/machine-representation-source-extraction-v0.md.
Completed machine-representation-source-gap-task-queue-v0 as disease-programs/multiple-myeloma/public-tasks/machine-representation-source-gap-task-queue-v0.md.
Completed machine-representation-source-gap-issue-draft-packet-v0 as disease-programs/multiple-myeloma/public-tasks/issue-drafts/machine-representation-source-gap-issue-draft-packet-v0.md.

Completed machine-representation-implementation-completion-audit-v0 and activated machine-representation-public-scope-human-gate-blocker-v0 because no autonomous public-safe machine-representation implementation item remains.
Marked complete-human-gated after the completion audit; later MRD geometry work did not clear this gate.

phases.label: MRD Resistance Geometry Falsification v0
phases.goal: Record the human-selected public-safe MRD resistance geometry falsification lane after the machine-representation human gate, without clearing that gate or creating clinical claims.
requirements: Use public sources only and preserve the no-human-outreach execution path.
Close the remaining MRD geometry second-source coverage gaps without upgrading clinical claims.
Add falsification matrix, transition model, hypothesis candidate ledger, benchmark fixtures, and invariant checker.
Do not use real case data, patient-specific interpretation, advice, ranking, outreach, private correspondence, or cure claims.
success_criteria: All ten MRD geometry buckets have at least two public extraction records for v0 navigation.
Every geometry bucket has weaken, split, merge, and block tests.
Candidate hypotheses remain research-only and link to transition rules and prohibited inferences.
Benchmark fixtures and invariant checks fail closed without real case data.
The machine-representation human gate remains explicitly unresolved.
plans: Completed by PR #55 as a public-safe research substrate phase.
This phase did not clear expert-review, human-review, private-lab, clinical-team, model-governance, legal, regulatory, or publication gates.

phases.label: Machine Representation Expert Validation Execution v0
phases.goal: Resume actual external machine-representation expert-validation execution only after human authorization, while keeping outreach, public issue operations, response intake, expert-review claim upgrades, private correspondence, and clinical interpretation blocked until then.
requirements: Do not treat MRD geometry falsification as expert review or human gate clearance.
Do not send outreach, copy private correspondence, request private material, or publish private responses without explicit human authorization.
Allowed autonomous preparation is limited to public-safe issue/status tracking, wording pressure tests, and source-gap disposition scaffolding.
Preserve no advice, no ranking, no patient matching, no clinical validation, and no cure-claim boundaries.
success_criteria: Human authorization either permits a no-outreach public-safe issue/status execution pass or explicitly keeps the phase blocked.
Machine-representation source-gap expert-validation items are tracked without private correspondence or clinical claims.
Any future expert response remains represented as safe public disposition until permission and source context exist.
plans: No-outreach prep completed as machine-representation-expert-validation-no-outreach-prep-v0.
Actual external expert validation remains blocked until human authorization.

phases.label: Machine Representation Expert Validation No-Outreach Prep v0
phases.goal: Complete the autonomous public-safe prep portion by mapping source-gap tasks to pressure questions without outreach, issue creation, response intake, expert-review substitution, claim upgrade, or advice.
requirements: Use only public artifacts and source-gap task records.
Do not open issues, send outreach, request private material, ingest expert responses, or claim expert review.
Preserve no-advice, no-ranking, no-matching, no-publication, and no-cure boundaries.
success_criteria: A public-safe no-outreach execution packet exists with metadata and catalog coverage.
Source-gap tasks map to sharper pressure questions and blocked inferences.
ORP state keeps external expert-validation execution and outreach blocked.
plans: Completed as Machine Representation Expert Validation No-Outreach Execution Packet v0.

phases.label: Machine Representation Source-Gap Internal Extraction v0
phases.goal: Complete a no-outreach, public-source-only extraction pass for machine-representation source-gap pressure points, mapping therapy-exposure context and validation-governance anchors while preserving the fusion-architecture source-context-needed blocker.
requirements: Use only public sources and public repo artifacts.
Do not open issues, send outreach, tag reviewers, request private material, copy private correspondence, ingest response content, or claim expert review.

Keep therapy-exposure anchors descriptive only: no sequencing, access, eligibility, refractory assessment, toxicity management, treatment advice, trial advice, ranking, or patient-specific response linkage.
Use model-governance and reporting sources only as validation-pressure anchors, not as model validation, clinical utility, benchmark evidence, deployment readiness, or regulatory clearance.
Keep fusion architecture source-context-needed unless direct public method sources are extracted later.

requirements: Use only public sources and public repo artifacts.

requirements: Use only public sources and public repo artifacts.

phases.label: Machine Representation Expert Validation Human Authorization Blocker v0
phases.goal: Block actual external expert-validation execution, outreach, private correspondence, issue operations, response intake, expert-review claim upgrades, real quality review, and clinical interpretation unless a human explicitly authorizes scope or selects a new named no-outreach public-source phase.
requirements: Do not contact reviewers, tag people, open new issues, update issue state, copy private correspondence, or ingest response content.
Keep all expert-review, clinical, private-lab, legal, regulatory, and publication gates explicit.
Allow only a newly named no-outreach public-source extraction phase or public-safe status-prep item to proceed autonomously.
Do not review real reports, scans, biopsies, raw values, private records, or exact person-linked timing in public.
success_criteria: The blocker is the active ORP state after no-outreach prep.
The prior machine-representation expert-validation execution item remains blocked for actual external validation.
No artifact language implies expert-review completion, endorsement, consensus, clinical validity, publication readiness, advice, ranking, matching, or cure.
plans: Residual-disease modality-discordance source extraction and assay/specimen quality failure-mode checklist are complete and did not clear the expert-validation gate.
Recommended next no-outreach public-source successor, if selected, is measurement-state-refusal-fixture-extension-v0.
